context:
Two weeks until the fintech screen. Adapted plan: a focused 2‑week schedule that fits short morning sessions and a couple of longer evening blocks each week. Mornings ~30–40 minutes; evenings ~90–120 minutes; weekends include longer blocks. Week structure (Week 1):
- Mon (AM 30–40m / no evening)
- Tue (AM 30–40m / Evening 90–120m)
- Wed (AM 30–40m / no evening)
- Thu (AM 30–40m / Evening 90–120m)
- Fri (AM 30–40m / no evening)
- Sat (AM 45m / Evening 120–180m)
- Sun (AM 30–40m / Evening 60–90m)

Week 2:
- Mon (AM 30–40m / no evening)
- Tue (AM 30–40m / Evening 90–120m)
- Wed (AM 30–40m / no evening)
- Thu (AM 30–40m / Evening 90–120m)
- Fri (AM 30–40m / no evening)
- Sat (AM 45m / Evening 120–180m)
- Sun (AM 30–40m / Evening 60–90m)

Session priorities:
- Morning micro‑sessions (30–40m): quick breathing & one‑line pitch, 20–25m focused micro exercise on paper/whiteboard (patterns: sliding window, hash map, two pointers, BFS), 5–10m quick review/write action item for evening.
- Evening longer blocks (90–120m): 5m warm‑up, 25m timed coding problem, 10–20m review & reimplementation, 30–40m micro‑design or Django/transactions/observability deep dive, 5–10m STAR story rehearsal, 5–10m log 1–3 follow‑ups.

Focus areas:
- Medium coding problems (paper/whiteboard)
- Idempotency/transactions/persistence for fintech
- 4–6 STAR stories with product ownership emphasis
- 2 full mock interviews

Recording & review:
- Record at least two full mocks (one in Week 1, one in Week 2) and review with rubric (clarify questions asked, approach, edge cases, complexity, code clarity, communication). Write 1 concrete fix per ≤3 score.

Interview logistics checklist:
- Finalize Sunday before interview: wired connection, headphones, resume PDF open, scratch paper/pen, camera/mic test, backup device logged in; one‑page cheat sheet with pitch, 4 STAR bullets, 3 interviewer questions, recovery lines.

Practice prompts:
- Coding (paper/whiteboard): dedupe transactions within sliding window; compute per‑user rolling totals with memory constraints; longest substring without repeating chars; BFS shortest path on a small grid/graph.
- Design (micro): idempotent payment API (idempotency keys vs server dedupe); reconciliation job matching internal ledger to bank statements; monitoring & rollback plan for payment feature.
- Production helpers: pseudo‑implement idempotency store interface and retry/backoff with dead‑letter handling.
- Behavioral: shipped feature with KPI impact; incident you led and MTTR reduction; cross‑team negotiation with Product/Compliance.

Recording & review rubric:
- Use a short rubric: questions asked (Y/N), approach correctness, edge cases, complexity, code clarity, communication; score ≤3 with one concrete fix.

Next steps options:
- Export this schedule into a day‑by‑day checklist

DB readiness emphasis (latest input):
- The job description specifically called out database performance and SQL querying, so prioritize practical skills you can show quickly in a 45‑minute screen: writing correct SQL, reasoning about indexes/query plans, and explaining production trade‑offs (partitioning, caching, migrations). Plan includes priority topics and drills and practical drills:
- Priority topics and what to practice (high impact)
- Practical drills (do these repeatedly)
- Concrete sample interview questions to practice
- Short checklist of quick facts to memorize and mention
- Production judgment demonstrations
- Two‑week tweak to your existing schedule (where to insert DB work)
- Example 2‑day focused plan for DB readiness
- Next steps suggestions

Two‑week tweak to your existing schedule (DB-specific):
- Replace or interleave some coding practice with DB drills:
  - 3× per week (evening blocks): do one DB drill (query writing + EXPLAIN) instead of a coding problem.
  - 1
